metadata.title: Audit a Claude Cowork Task Packet in Sixty Seconds
metadata.note: CLI video: 10-beat spine B00–B09. B04 is slate. No audio.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

One wrong folder permission and a Cowork session reads three years of personnel files. The task packet audit catches it in sixty seconds. Without it, you have handed a capable agent a vague mandate and broad permissions.

shot.notes: hook card: 'One wrong permission. Three years of personnel files.' — CRIMSON on 'wrong permission', teal on 'sixty seconds' audit time

beats.narration_text: Build task_packet_audit.py: reads a Cowork task packet from standard input and scores it on six dimensions — inputs named, forbidden actions explicit, irreversibles gated, output format specified, verification steps listed, no sensitive data.

props.command:
claude "Write task_packet_audit.py:
  reads Cowork task packet from stdin
  scores 6 dimensions with pass/fail:
    inputs_named
    forbidden_explicit
    irreversibles_gated
    output_format
    verification_steps
    no_sensitive_data
  one-sentence explanation for each fail"

beats.narration_text: Check the code. Sensitive data detection flags PII patterns and credential keywords. Irreversibles gated checks for explicit stop conditions on delete, overwrite, and send operations. Each dimension has its own pattern-match — none is a vague heuristic.

Run it on the book's bad task packet: full Documents folder, no forbidden actions listed. Six fails appear in sequence — inputs not named, forbidden not explicit, irreversibles not gated, output format missing, verification absent, sensitive data pattern found.

shot.notes: terminal: bad packet piped in -> six FAIL rows in CRIMSON, each with one-sentence explanation — bad packet text visible above

beats.narration_text: Add a seventh dimension: stop conditions defined. The book's opening-scene failure — no stop condition on the agent — is the one the sixth dimension cannot catch. Add it and retest.

props.command:
claude "update task_packet_audit.py:
  add 7th dimension: stop_conditions_defined
  pattern: 'stop when' or 'halt if'
  retest on both bad and good packets
  verify 7th catches opening-scene failure"

beats.narration_text: Bad packet: seven fails. Good packet: seven passes. The seventh dimension catches the opening-scene failure mode — no stop condition defined — that the original six missed. The audit is now complete.

shot.notes: terminal: bad packet 7 FAIL (CRIMSON), good packet 7 PASS (teal) — seventh row 'stop_conditions_defined' highlighted

beats.narration_text: The task packet is not bureaucracy — it is the specification that makes supervision possible. Without it, you have handed a capable agent a vague mandate and broad permissions. The audit makes that gap visible before the session begins.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'Vague mandate + broad permissions = unsupervisable agent' (CRIMSON) vs 'Seven-dimension packet = auditable scope' (teal)

beats.narration_text: Your move: write your next Cowork task as a plain-text packet and run it through the audit before starting. Next reel: the risk-tiered verification checklist — reading Claude's output is not reviewing it.

shot.notes: next-reel teaser: 'Next: Risk-Tiered Verification' — segment 06 of the CLAUDE series

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
